item.title: China’s Silver Imports Jump to Record on Retail and Solar Demand
item.description: China’s imports of silver surged to an all-time high in March as demand from retail investors and the country’s massive solar industry pushed purchases well above the seasonal average.

item.title: William Hill Owner Evoke in Takeover Talks With Bally’s Intralot
item.description: Bally’s Intralot SA is in talks to buy William Hill owner Evoke Plc in a proposed deal that could rescue the struggling British gambling firm.

item.title: Hedge Funds Turn Bullish on Cotton for First Time in Two Years
item.description: Money managers have turned net-bullish on cotton as surging oil prices tied to the war in Iran boost the appeal of natural fiber over increasingly expensive synthetics.

item.title: India Widens Russian Insurance List as Iran War Hurts Oil Flows
item.description: India allowed more Russian insurers to provide cover for vessels calling at its ports, while extending permits for others, as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz hits energy shipments from the Persian Gulf.

item.title: BHP Begins Review to Rank Unprofitable Australian Coal Mines
item.description: BHP Group and Mitsubishi Development Pty Ltd. have launched a review process to rank the financial health of their coal mines and assets in Queensland after raising concerns about the Australian state’s royalty regime.

item.title: How Niche Insurance Shielded Bad Bunny From Bad Weather
item.description: Bad Bunny had a problem. Days before the Puerto Rican mega-star was set to perform in Colombia earlier this year, the possibility of heavy rains threatened to turn three sold-out shows in Medellin into multimillion-dollar losses.

item.title: A Stock Trader’s Guide to a Fractured Economic World
item.description: Investors are set to pour more money into defense, energy and technology stocks as the Middle East war forces governments to prioritize security and become more self reliant.

item.title: Ninety One Sees South Africa Opportunities in War Volatility
item.description: Ninety One Plc remains constructive on South African equities, saying the war in Iran has created opportunities to buy stocks at prices that are lower than justified by their earnings outlook.

item.title: Hormuz Traffic at Standstill as US Vessel Seizure Widens Risk
item.description: Commercial traffic through the Strait of Hormuz is at a virtual standstill on Monday after a brief and confused reopening over the weekend ended with the first US seizure of an Iranian vessel — underscoring just how difficult it will be to restore activity in the vital strait.

item.title: Revolut CEO Storonsky Says Digital Bank’s IPO Is Two Years Out
item.description: Revolut Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Nik Storonsky said he wants to take the London-based digital bank public, but not until at least 2028, extending the timeline on one of Europe’s most anticipated offerings.

item.title: Stocks Set to Fall on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
Investors Bet ‘Peak Uncertainty’ Has Passed as Asia Stocks Climb
item.description: Asian stocks rose Monday as investors looked past renewed Middle East tensions over the weekend, shifting focus back to fundamentals and the prospect of further talks.

item.title: AirTrunk to Acquire Lumina CloudInfra to Expand in India
item.description: AirTrunk, a data center operator backed by Blackstone Inc., is buying Lumina CloudInfra to expand in India.

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Currency Is Undervalued by Nearly Half, Governor Says
item.description: Zimbabwe’s currency is undervalued by almost half, the governor of the central bank said, pointing to the foreign reserves and gold that back the unit.

item.title: Germany’s Sovereign Wealth Fund Is Dropping Weapons Exclusions
item.description: Germany’s sovereign wealth fund is abandoning long-held restrictions on investing in weapons manufacturers, as it responds to an increasingly tense geopolitical reality.

item.title: Iran War Deepens China’s Dependence on the US for Niche Gas
item.description: China is set to import a record volume of US ethane this month as petrochemical producers seek alternative feedstocks for their operations after the war in the Middle East choked off crucial supplies.

item.title: Iron Ore Gains With China Demand Resilient Ahead of May Day
item.description: Iron ore rose as Chinese demand remained strong ahead of the May Day holidays while near-term supply was constrained.

item.title: AirAsia Tests Market With Deutsche-Led $230 Million Private Debt
item.description: Deutsche Bank AG is marketing a $230 million private-credit deal for Malaysian budget airline AirAsia Aviation Group, according to people familiar with the matter, testing investor demand for the carrier amid rising fuel prices.

item.title: Taiwan Weighs US Dollar Dividend Payouts for Listed Companies
item.description: Taiwan is weighing a proposal to permit listed companies to distribute dividends in US dollars, a change from the rule requiring payouts in the local currency, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Markets Get in Tune to the Strait and Narrow
item.description: Earnings hopes counter the volatile lack of message discipline by the US and Iran.

item.title: Siemens Warns EU’s AI Rules Will Deter Investment in Europe
item.description: Siemens AG will prioritize artificial-intelligence investments in the US and China if the European Union doesn’t adapt its restrictive regulations, according to Chief Executive Officer Roland Busch.

item.title: China’s Netflix iQiyi Goes All-In on AI Content in Big Overhaul
item.description: IQiyi Inc. expects AI to create a big chunk of its films and shows virtually from scratch someday soon, a monumental industry shift that spurred the Netflix-style streaming service to begin the biggest corporate overhaul in its 16-year history.

item.title: Singapore Is Procuring More LNG as Iran War Cuts Some Supply
item.description: Singapore is getting additional liquefied natural gas from outside of the Middle East, as the war in Iran chokes supply from the region, according to a government body.

item.title: Malaysia Cautious as War-Led Inflation to Hit Later This Year
item.description: Malaysia is bracing for a delayed uptick in inflation as the impact of the Iran war filters through to consumer prices later this year, according to Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

item.title: New Zealand Exports Hit Monthly Record on Dairy, China Demand
item.description: New Zealand’s exports hit an all-time high in March as the trade-dependent economy offset the early impact of the Iran war with bumper dairy sales overseas.

item.title: Takaichi’s Ratings Stay High With Some Wavering Amid Iran Impact
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s popularity ratings remained high by historical standards despite a slight dip in some polls as the public weighed her handling of the impact of the war in Iran.

item.title: Major Japanese Life Insurer to Slow Buying of Domestic Debt
item.description: Fukoku Mutual Life Insurance Co. plans to slow purchases of Japanese government debt this fiscal year as it sees limited upside in super-long bond yields.

item.title: Indian Stocks’ Rally Faces Test as Oil Jumps on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
item.description: Nifty capped a second straight weekly advance on Friday.

item.title: China’s Government Urges ‘Every Effort’ to Curb Solar Capacity
item.description: Chinese authorities have called for “every effort” to strengthen capacity controls in the solar industry, as the sector continues to struggle with excess production.

item.title: Singapore Urges Banks to Fix Security Gaps Amid Mythos AI Fears
item.description: Singapore’s financial regulator is urging banks to plug their cybersecurity holes, as concerns over Anthropic PBC’s latest AI model Mythos spread to Asia.
